content.content:
1	# Marketify — Technical Architecture Document
2	
3	**Scope:** Backend, data, APIs, workflows. UI/UX is separate (see `design.md`).
4	**Stack:** Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase (Postgres, Storage, Edge Functions, Realtime) + **Apify** (two actors: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper`, `apify/instagram-scraper`).
5	**Posture:** Opinionated. Where the PRD is silent, a default is chosen with justification.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. Design Decisions
10	
11	The table below captures every non-obvious architectural choice. Each row is a committed default that the rest of the document relies on — if you need to revisit one, expect ripple effects in §4 (schema), §5 (edge functions), and `design.md` (UX).
12	
13	### 1.1 Identity & Auth
14	
15	| Area | Decision |
16	|---|---|
17	| Metric collection | **Apify scrapers, no OAuth.** Creator signs up with role + username + TikTok handle and/or IG handle. Server kicks an Apify actor to fetch metrics asynchronously. Handle ownership is unverified in MVP. |
18	| Username uniqueness | `username` is the unique display identifier (citext). `(platform, handle)` is also unique across `social_links` — first creator to register a handle owns it. Collisions surface with a "handle already registered" error; we do not verify ownership. |
19	| Creator + lister on same account | One role per account. Dual-role users make two accounts. |
20	| Lister identity | Self-declared: username + email + org name. KYC is post-MVP. |
21	| Handle impersonation | No ownership verification in MVP — first registrant wins. Impersonation is a manual-report problem; accept until it becomes real abuse. |
22	
23	### 1.2 Listing Lifecycle
24	
25	| Area | Decision |
26	|---|---|
27	| States | `draft → active → (paused \| closed) → archived`. No `expired` state. |
28	| Auto-expiration | None. `end_date` column retained for v1.1; listers close listings manually. |
29	| Submission cap | `max_submissions` nullable. Listers close manually when they're done — no auto-close trigger in v1. |
30	| Application uniqueness | One active application per `(creator, listing)`. Partial unique index. |
31	| Cascade-on-edit | Editing any versioned field — price, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos, max_submissions — bumps a version and cascade-cancels pending applications with `cancelled_listing_edit`. Title/description edits do NOT cascade. |
32	
33	### 1.3 Applications & Submissions
34	
35	| Area | Decision |
36	|---|---|
37	| Withdraw | Creator can withdraw from `pending` → `withdrawn`. |
38	| Eligibility drift between apply and approve | Re-check at approve-time. If now-ineligible, lister receives `409 INELIGIBLE_NOW` with `override_allowed=true`. No re-check at submit-time. |
39	| Videos per submission | 1..N video URLs per submission. `min_videos` post-condition (default 1). |
40	| Revision request | Binary approve/reject only. Column reserved for v1.1. |
41	| Reapplication | After `rejected` / `cancelled_listing_edit`: allowed if listing still active. After `withdrawn`: allowed immediately. |
42	| Submission reuse | A single video URL may be submitted to multiple campaigns. Surfaced to the lister via `ReuseBadge` (design §4.6) but not blocked. |
43	| Disputes | Append-only `events` table; support email only in MVP. |
44	| Pending apps/submissions expiry | No auto-expire. Rows stay in-inbox until explicitly acted on. |
45	
46	### 1.4 Money
47	
48	| Area | Decision |
49	|---|---|
50	| Payments | No real money in MVP. `price` is display-only. Off-platform handshake. `payment_status` column stubbed for v2. |
51	
52	### 1.5 Notifications
53	
54	| Area | Decision |
55	|---|---|
56	| Events | Application approved/rejected/cancelled; submission approved/rejected; listing version changed; metrics refresh failed. |
57	| Channels | In-app inbox + Supabase Realtime. Push deferred to v1.1. |
58	| Transport | Realtime on `applications`, `submissions`, `notifications`. Poll fallback on app resume. |
59	
60	### 1.6 Metrics / Apify
61	
62	| Area | Decision |
63	|---|---|
64	| Rate limits | Apify costs real money per scrape. Server-side throttle: manual refresh once per 6h per `(user, platform)`. Background refresh daily via `pg_cron` for all linked handles with `fetched_at < now() - 24h`. |
65	| Scrape failures | `metric_snapshots.status = fresh \| stale \| refreshing \| failed`. "Outdated" badge if `fetched_at > 7d`. After 3 consecutive failed scrapes, `social_link.status = failed_fetch`, prompt creator to re-enter handle. |
66	| Video integrity | Validate via TikTok/IG oEmbed at submission time (`422` if 404). Nightly sweep re-validates `approved` submissions — flags but doesn't change state. |
67	| Post-submission performance | Out of MVP. Later: an Apify actor per submitted video to pull current view count. |
68	
69	### 1.7 Security / Abuse
70	
71	| Area | Decision |
72	|---|---|
73	| Spam/illegal listings | `report_listing` button → admin table. Admin reviews via Supabase Studio. |
74	| Submission cross-posting | Flag via `ReuseBadge`, don't hard-block. |
75	
76	---
77	
78	## 2. State Machines
79	
80	Notation: `transition [actor: user|lister|system|time]`.
81	
82	### 2.1 Listing
83	
84	```
85	(init) ──create[lister]──> draft
86	draft ──publish[lister]──> active
87	active ──pause[lister]──> paused
88	paused ──resume[lister]──> active
89	active ──close[lister]──> closed
90	closed ──archive[lister]──> archived
91	paused ──archive[lister]──> archived
92	
93	Side-effect on edit mutating price/pre-cond/post-cond/samples/max_submissions while active:
94	  version++ AND cascade-cancel pending applications (see §2.3)
95	```
96

98

101	```
102	(init) ──submit[creator, gated by eligibility]──> pending
103	pending ──approve[lister]──> approved
104	pending ──reject[lister]──> rejected
105	pending ──withdraw[creator]──> withdrawn
106	pending ──listing_version_bump[system]──> cancelled_listing_edit
107	pending ──listing_archived[system]──> cancelled_listing_closed
108	approved ──listing_archived[system]──> cancelled_listing_closed
109	```

112	
113	### 2.3 Submission
114	
115	```
116	(init, requires application.status=approved) ──create[creator]──> pending
117	pending ──approve[lister]──> approved
118	pending ──reject[lister]──> rejected
119	```
120	
121	No `revision_requested` in v1. No time-based expiration.
122	
123	### 2.4 Social Link
124	
125	```
126	(init) ──handle_added[creator]──> linked       (triggers initial Apify scrape)
127	linked ──scrape_success[system]──> linked
128	linked ──scrape_fail x3[system]──> failed_fetch
129	failed_fetch ──handle_reentered[creator]──> linked
130	linked ──unlink[creator]──> unlinked
131	```
132	
133	### 2.5 Metric Snapshot
134	
135	```
136	(init, on link or refresh) ──apify_run_started[system]──> refreshing
137	refreshing ──success[system|apify_webhook]──> fresh
138	refreshing ──fail[system]──> failed
139	fresh ──age > 24h[time]──> stale
140	stale ──run_started[creator_manual | system_daily]──> refreshing
141	failed ──retry[system|creator]──> refreshing
142	```
143	
144	Rule: eligibility filter uses `fresh` OR `stale`, never `failed`. UI warns if `stale`.

content.content:
1543	## 5. API / Edge Function Spec Sheet
1544	
1545	**Transport mix:**
1546	- **Direct PostgREST** for RLS-safe reads (feed browse, notifications inbox).
1547	- **Edge Functions** for (a) external Apify calls, (b) multi-table atomic writes, (c) business logic, (d) JWT issuance, (e) webhook receivers.
1548	- **Realtime channels** on `applications`, `submissions`, `notifications`.
1549	
1550	Role = custom-JWT `role` claim.
1551	
1552	### 5.1 Auth
1553	
1554	**`POST /auth/signup-creator`**
1555	```ts
1556	interface Req {
1557	  username: string;
1558	  tiktok_handle?: string;
1559	  instagram_handle?: string;        // at least one of the two
1560	  display_name?: string;
1561	  country?: string;
1562	}
1563	interface Res {
1564	  user_id: string;
1565	  jwt: string;
1566	  social_links: { id: string; platform: Platform; handle: string; status: 'linked' }[];
1567	  initial_scrape_queued: boolean;
1568	}
1569	```
1570	**Transaction boundary:** the identity rows (`users`, `creator_profiles`, `social_links`, and pre-created `refreshing` `metric_snapshots` rows) commit in a single Postgres transaction. Apify runs are dispatched **after** the transaction commits — a dispatch failure does NOT roll back the account. The creator gets a working account, the `social_links` row, and a `refreshing` snapshot; the UI displays "Couldn't fetch metrics — we'll retry automatically" and the daily cron picks the link up within 24h. This is intentional: Apify outages shouldn't block signup.
1571	
1572	**Per-run outcomes after the commit** (handled inline in the edge function before returning):
1573	- If `.call(input, { waitSecs: 60 })` returns with `status: 'SUCCEEDED'` and items → parse + UPDATE snapshot to `fresh` inline. Counts toward the response's `initial_scrape_queued = false` flag per-platform.
1574	- If it returns with `status: 'RUNNING'` (inside-wait timeout) → webhook completes later. `initial_scrape_queued = true`.
1575	- If it throws (rate limit, 4xx/5xx from Apify) → UPDATE snapshot to `failed` with `error_message`, increment `social_links.fail_count`, return normally. The UI chips the failed mode as "Couldn't fetch" with a retry button.
1576	
1577	Signup is idempotent on `(username)` — a duplicate call returns `409 USERNAME_TAKEN` before any Apify dispatch.
1578	
1579	**Per-IP throttle:** the edge function computes `sha256(ip + SIGNUP_SALT)` and inserts a `signup_attempts` row. If ≥5 attempts from the same `ip_hash` occurred in the prior 60 minutes (regardless of success/failure), return `429 TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS` with `retry_after_sec`. Salt rotates quarterly.
1580	
1581	Errors: `400 MISSING_HANDLE`, `409 USERNAME_TAKEN`, `409 HANDLE_ALREADY_REGISTERED`, `429 TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS`.
1582	
1583	**`POST /auth/signup-lister`**
1584	```ts
1585	interface Req { username: string; email: string; org_name: string; website_url?: string; }
1586	interface Res { user_id: string; jwt: string; }
1587	```
1588	Same per-IP throttle as `signup-creator` (shared `signup_attempts` table).
1589	Errors: `409 USERNAME_TAKEN`, `409 EMAIL_TAKEN`, `429 TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS`.
1590	
1591	**`POST /auth/signout`**
1592	```ts
1593	interface Req { session_id: string; }
1594	interface Res { ok: true; }
1595	```
1596	Inserts a row into `revoked_sessions` with `expires_at = <jwt exp claim>`. Edge middleware caches the revocation set in-memory (5-min TTL; `LISTEN revoked_sessions_invalidate` pushes inserts live, with force-reload on reconnect — see §15c comments). JWTs with `session_id` in the set are rejected with `401`. Max stale-revocation window = 5 min (TTL) worst-case after a container cold-start that misses a NOTIFY during the one-request reload gap; typical window is zero because NOTIFY is synchronous.
1597	
1598	### 5.2 Social Handles & Metrics
1599	
1600	**`POST /social/add-handle`** — creator links an additional platform
1601	```ts
1602	interface Req { platform: Platform; handle: string; }
1603	interface Res { social_link_id: string; scrape_run_id: string; }
1604	```
1605	Side effects: insert `social_links` + kick initial Apify run (async, webhook-completed).
1606	Errors: `409 HANDLE_ALREADY_REGISTERED`, `409 PLATFORM_ALREADY_LINKED`.
1607	
1608	**`POST /social/update-handle`** — creator changed their username on the platform
1609	```ts
1610	interface Req { social_link_id: string; new_handle: string; }
1611	interface Res { ok: true; scrape_run_id: string; }
1612	```
1613	Side effects: update handle + trigger fresh scrape. Old snapshots retained.
1614	Errors: `409 HANDLE_ALREADY_REGISTERED`.
1615	
1616	**`POST /social/unlink`**
1617	```ts
1618	interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
1619	interface Res { ok: true; }
1620	```
1621	Errors: `409 LAST_LINK` (creator must keep ≥1 linked platform).
1622	
1623	**`POST /metrics/refresh`** — creator-triggered, rate-limited
1624	```ts
1625	interface Req { social_link_id: string; }
1626	interface Res {
1627	  snapshot_ids: string[];  // 1 for TikTok, 2 for Instagram (details + posts)
1628	  status: 'queued' | 'already_fresh';
1629	  retry_after_sec?: number;
1630	}
1631	```
1632	Side effects: check 6h throttle on `social_links.last_scrape_attempt_at`; for each scrape_mode owed by the platform, insert `metric_snapshots(status=refreshing, scrape_mode=...)` and start the matching Apify run with our webhook.
1633	
1634	| Platform | scrape_modes triggered | Actor runs |
1635	|---|---|---|
1636	| `tiktok` | `tiktok_profile` | `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` × 1 |
1637	| `instagram` | `ig_details`, `ig_posts` | `apify/instagram-scraper` × 2 (different `resultsType`) |
1638	
1639	Errors: `429 RATE_LIMIT` (with `retry_after_sec`), `502 APIFY_ERROR`.
1640	
1641	**Internal cron-invoked function `cron-refresh-metrics`**
1642	- Runs daily (scheduled via Supabase `pg_cron` → edge function).
1643	- Picks up to `APIFY_DAILY_BUDGET` links with `metrics_fetched_at < now() - 24h AND status != 'failed_fetch'`.
1644	- Fires the appropriate scrape_modes per link (TikTok=1 run, IG=2 runs). All async with our webhook.
1645	- **Overlap guard.** Apify slowdowns or budget pressure can cause a run to take longer than 24h, so the next cron firing could overlap its predecessor. The edge function opens its DB transaction with:
1646	  ```sql
1647	  -- 0xC17DEF1E15 is an arbitrary 64-bit key reserved for this cron. Returns
1648	  -- false immediately if another session holds it — the function then exits
1649	  -- with a `cron_refresh_skipped_overlap` event (for ops visibility) and no
1650	  -- Apify runs are queued.
1651	  select pg_try_advisory_lock(hashtextextended('cron-refresh-metrics', 0));
1652	  ```
1653	  The lock auto-releases at txn end (session-scoped via `pg_advisory_lock` would leak on container crash; `pg_try_advisory_xact_lock` is safer). This guarantees at-most-one active refresh cron, regardless of how far behind the previous one ran. The 24h cadence combined with the 10-min `fail-stuck-refreshing` janitor means no single slow run can stall the pipeline indefinitely.
1654	
1655	### 5.3 Apify Webhook Receiver
1656	
1657	**`POST /webhooks/apify`** — called by Apify when a run finishes
1658	```ts
1659	interface Req {  // Apify payload + our payloadTemplate vars
1660	  eventType:
1661	    | 'ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED'
1662	    | 'ACTOR.RUN.FAILED'
1663	    | 'ACTOR.RUN.TIMED_OUT'
1664	    | 'ACTOR.RUN.ABORTED';
1665	  resource: { id: string; defaultDatasetId: string; status: string; actId: string; };
1666	  // injected via payloadTemplate when we started the run:
1667	  social_link_id: string;
1668	  scrape_mode: 'tiktok_profile' | 'ig_details' | 'ig_posts';
1669	  run_id: string;
1670	  status: string;
1671	  dataset_id: string;
1672	}
1673	interface Res { ok: true; }
1674	```
1675	Security: verify `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret` header (constant-time compare) against `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET`. Idempotent by `run_id` (partial unique index on `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id`).
1676	
1677	Flow:
1678	1. Look up the pre-created `metric_snapshots` row by `apify_run_id`. If missing (rare edge case — initial insert failed), create one in `refreshing` state so later steps are idempotent.
1679	2. If `eventType !== 'ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED'`: prepare `status_next=failed`, fill `error_message`, short-circuit to step 5.
1680	3. Fetch dataset items via `client.dataset(dataset_id).listItems({ clean: true })`.
1681	4. Parse per-scrape_mode (see §3e table) — set `follower_count`/`avg_views_last_10`/etc. columns the mode owns, leave others null.
1682	5. **Guarded terminal-state UPDATE** — crucial for Apify's retry semantics, which can deliver `SUCCEEDED` first and a retried `ABORTED` for the same `run_id` later (or vice versa). The update only transitions OUT OF `refreshing`, never between terminal states:
1683	   ```sql
1684	   update public.metric_snapshots
1685	      set status        = :status_next,         -- 'fresh' | 'failed'
1686	          raw_payload   = :items,
1687	          error_message = :error_message,
1688	          -- fetched_at stamped at run-completion, not at webhook-arrival,
1689	          -- so retries don't shift the timestamp forward.
1690	          fetched_at    = :apify_finished_at
1691	    where apify_run_id = :run_id
1692	      and status       = 'refreshing';          -- guard: do nothing if already terminal
1693	   ```
1694	   If zero rows are affected, log a `webhook_duplicate` event and return `200 OK` — idempotent no-op. The first terminal signal wins; later retries are ignored. This keeps a fresh snapshot from being clobbered by a retried-ABORTED.
1695	6. Trigger fires `denorm_metrics()` → updates only the `creator_profiles` columns owned by this `scrape_mode`. (Note: `denorm_metrics()` already short-circuits when the new row's status is not `fresh` — see §15 trigger — so the no-op guard in step 5 also keeps the denorm path clean.)
1696	7. On failure: increment `social_links.fail_count`; if ≥3 consecutive failures across all scrape_modes → `social_links.status=failed_fetch` + notify creator. On success: reset `social_links.fail_count = 0` so the consecutive-failure counter only reflects *uninterrupted* failures.
1697	
1698	**IG consistency note:** Because IG needs two runs per refresh, `creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` will bump *twice* per refresh (once per webhook). The UI treats "fresh" as `min(metrics_fetched_at_across_modes)`. If one run fails and the other succeeds, the creator still sees a partial update — the failed mode's old value remains stale-but-shown, with a subtle warning chip. (Detail reconciled via `metric_snapshots.status` per mode.)
1699

1720	**`POST /listings`**
1721	```ts
1722	interface Req {

1816	
1817	| Channel | Who | Events |
1818	|---|---|---|

Channel filters in the client are an optimization, not a security boundary. Manual test plan for launch: subscribe from Creator A's JWT to a channel filtered on Creator B's id, perform a write against Creator B's data, assert zero events delivered. Automate this in the integration suite.

1830	## 6. Eligibility Evaluation Engine
1831	
1832	### 6.1 Condition Catalog
1833	
1834	| metric | platform | operator | threshold |
1835	|---|---|---|---|
1836	| `min_followers` | tt \| ig | gte | numeric |
1837	| `min_avg_views_last_n` | tt \| ig | gte | numeric |
1838	| `min_total_likes` | tt | gte | numeric |

1841	
1842	### 6.2 Evaluator Signature
1843	
1844	```ts
1845	type Metric = 'min_followers' | 'min_avg_views_last_n' | 'min_total_likes' | 'min_videos_posted' | 'verified_only';

## Focused Research Summary for US-046: Submission Composer Screen

### 1. **Spec Pointers (Design & Product)**

- **`docs/design.md` §4.4 (lines 483–513)**: Wireframe for Submission Composer — Platform segmented control (TikTok/Instagram), URL input field, live preview (thumbnail + caption), post-condition checklist (2-state: checked/unchecked), "Send for review" CTA. Companion section §4.6 (lines 549–610) shows the lister-side Submission Review with 3-state checklist for comparison.
- **`docs/design.md` §1.4 (lines 82–110)**: Motion language — spring presets (soft, snappy, bouncy), timing presets (micro 150ms, short 220ms, medium 320ms). Submit-on-success uses a success toast with 2.5s auto-dismiss (line 439).
- **`docs/product-plan.md` Step 11 (lines 185–186)**: Submission composer + review live in a single build step alongside notifications — no prior/blocking dependency.
- **Route location**: `/(creator)/applications/[id]/submit` per design.md §2.2 table (line 153) — the creator navigates from Application Detail when approved.

### 2. **Existing Route-Param Screen Pattern**

**Reference: `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx`** (US-040 Campaign Detail)

- **Route params**: `useLocalSearchParams<{ id: string }>()` (line 12, 84)
- **Data fetch**: `supabase.functions.invoke('get-listing-detail', { body: { listing_id: id } })` (lines 95–98). Custom-JWT injected via `marketifyFetch` wrapper in `src/lib/supabase.ts` (lines 24–32).
- **Load state machine**: `{ kind: 'loading' | 'error' | 'ok' }` (lines 78–81). Error codes: `'not_found' | 'generic'` (line 80).
- **Button gating + disabled style**: `FooterCta` function (lines 355–425) — conditional renders based on `listing.status`, `eligibility.eligible`, `eligibility.has_active_application`. "Apply" button disabled until conditions met (line 405–410).
- **Toast + navigation-on-success pattern** (lines 142–154): `useToast().show({message, variant})` → `setTimeout(350) → router.replace('/(creator)/applications')` to allow sheet animation to settle.
- **Imports**:
  - `useLocalSearchParams` from `'expo-router'`
  - `useToast` from `'@/components/primitives/Toast'`
  - `supabase` from `'@/lib/supabase'`
  - `Database` types from `'@/types/supabase'`

### 3. **Supabase Client / Edge Function Invocation**

**Pattern from `src/lib/supabase.ts` (lines 24–45)**:
- Custom `marketifyFetch` wrapper intercepts every Supabase request and adds `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.
- `supabase` client reads the JWT from `getCachedToken()` (cached in-memory by `AuthProvider`).
- For edge functions: `supabase.functions.invoke<ResponseType>('function-name', { body: {...} })` returns `{ data, error }`. Error handling via `error.context as Response` then `.json()` for the body (Codebase Pattern #98).

**For authenticated Realtime** (if needed later): `await supabase.realtime.setAuth(getCachedToken())` before `channel().subscribe()` (Codebase Pattern #100 in progress.txt).

### 4. **oEmbed Integration**

**Exists in repo**: `supabase/functions/_shared/oembed.ts`

- **Exports**: `classifyVideoUrl(url: string)` → `{ platform, externalId } | null`; `fetchTikTokOembed(url)` → `{ thumbnailUrl, authorHandle, raw }`.
- **Platform detection** (lines 44–71): TikTok canonical + shortlinks (vm.tiktok.com, vt.tiktok.com, tiktok.com/t/...), Instagram (instagram.com/p/, reel/, tv/).
- **TikTok oEmbed** (lines 73–106): `GET https://www.tiktok.com/oembed?url=<share_url>` (no auth). Returns `404` → null, `4xx/5xx` → throw, success → `{ thumbnail_url, author_unique_id, ... }`.
- **Instagram oEmbed** (line 27–28): Regex validation only (no Meta token provisioned in v1).
- **Client-side usage**: oEmbed is called server-side in the `submit-video` edge function (US-045); the client validates shape via `classifyVideoUrl` for immediate feedback, then the edge function confirms with TikTok oEmbed before creating the submission.

### 5. **submit-video Edge Function Contract (US-045)**

**File**: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` (lines 1–287)

**Handler signature** (line 107): `Deno.serve(async (req) => { ... })`

**Request body** (lines 72–76):
```ts
{ application_id: string, video_url: string, post_condition_affirmations: { [condition_id]: boolean } }
```

**Response shapes**:
- **200**: `{ submission_id: string, platform: 'tiktok'|'instagram' }`
- **400 INVALID_REQUEST**: body shape error or URL length > 2048 chars
- **403 FORBIDDEN**: non-creator role or APPLICATION_NOT_APPROVED
- **404 APPLICATION_NOT_FOUND**
- **409 SUBMISSION_EXISTS**: duplicate submission on same application
- **422 INVALID_VIDEO_URL**: URL doesn't match TikTok/IG patterns or TikTok oEmbed returns 404
- **422 INCOMPLETE_AFFIRMATIONS**: `{ error, missing_condition_ids: string[] }` — any post-condition not in affirmations as `true`
- **502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE**: TikTok oEmbed network/upstream error
- **500 DB_ERROR, SERVER_MISCONFIGURED**

**Key logic** (lines 157–237):
- Classify URL + validate shape (lines 157–160)
- Fetch application + creator ownership check (lines 172–187)
- Fetch post-conditions for the pinned `listing_version_id` (lines 197–206)
- Validate affirmations completeness (lines 208–217)
- TikTok oEmbed call (lines 223–237)
- RPC `submit_video_rpc` with row-lock serialization (lines 239–249)

### 6. **Post-Conditions Data Structure**

**How existing screens read conditions**:
- `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` calls `get-listing-detail` edge function (US-039), which fetches `listing_conditions` (line 199) and splits by `kind: 'pre' | 'post'` on the client (lines 257–264).
- **ConditionRow type** (line 55): `{ id, listing_version_id, kind, metric, platform?, numeric_threshold?, bool_threshold?, text_threshold?, operator, required }`.
- **Post-condition labels** (lines 590–616): `post_family_friendly`, `post_must_mention`, `post_must_tag_account`, `post_min_video_duration_sec`, etc. (see `Database['public']['Enums']['condition_kind']` = `'pre' | 'post'`).

**For US-046 composer**: Fetch conditions via the same `get-listing-detail` edge function or a dedicated `get-application/:id` endpoint (not yet implemented — may need to add). Conditions are pinned to `application.listing_version_id` (see submit-video line 192).

### 7. **Toast Primitive (US-028)**

**File**: `src/components/primitives/Toast.tsx` (lines 1–254)

**API**:
```ts
useToast(): { show: (payload: ToastPayload) => void; hide: () => void }
ToastPayload = { message: string; variant?: 'success'|'error'|'info'; duration?: number /* ms, default 2500 */ }
```

**Usage pattern** (from listing detail, line 144):
```ts
const { show: showToast } = useToast();
showToast({ message: 'Application sent.', variant: 'success' });
```

**Mount**: `<ToastProvider>` wraps the app once near the root (probably in `app/_layout.tsx`). Progress bar animates from full width to 0 over the duration.

### 8. **Post-Submit Navigation**

**Fallback route**: `/(creator)/submissions` (the "Submitted" tab, currently a placeholder per `app/(creator)/submissions.tsx` lines 3–10 — US-047 will replace it with a real list).

**Spec route**: Per design.md §2.2, creator navigates from "Submitted" tab after submit. The exact path after US-047 ships is `/(creator)/submissions`; until then, fallback to `/(creator)/applications` or re-route to home `/(creator)/feed`.

**Pattern**: Same as listing detail's post-apply (lines 153–154) — `setTimeout(350) → router.replace(target)`.

### 9. **Codebase Patterns (from progress.txt grep results)**

Key excerpts:

- **Pattern #97 (Functions.invoke error handling)**: `{ data: null, error: FunctionsHttpError }` where `error.context` is the raw Response. Type-narrow on `'status' in ctx` and `typeof (ctx as {json?}).json === 'function'`, then `await (ctx as Response).json()`. Do NOT clone — stream is unread. Fallback to generic toast on non-JSON body. *(Used in signup-lister and reused verbatim in edge-function callers)*

- **Pattern #100 (Realtime auth under custom JWT)**: `await supabase.realtime.setAuth(getCachedToken()); channel = supabase.channel(...).subscribe()` with a `cancelled` flag because setAuth is async.

- **Pattern #82 (Rapid-fire gesture locks)**: `useRef(false)` for synchronous guard (read+write in same tick) when callback is invoked multiple times and has stale-capture risk.

- **Press feedback motion**: Every pressable should scale 0.97 + collapse shadow on pressIn via `useAnimatedStyle` + `useSharedValue`, using `withSpring` with the motion preset.

- **Button disable gating**: Defense-in-depth — guard BOTH `handlePressIn` and `handlePressOut` even when `Pressable.disabled` is set (transient window between mode flips).
